title: Allow to define how many values can be selected in a dropdown column

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

it would be really nice if I were able to limit how many values in a dropdown column someone is able to assign to an entry e.g. no more than 1, no more than 2, etc.

Right now there doesn’t appear to be a way to limit for someone to assign as many values as they want. This also causes issues with forms where I’d like someone to pick exactly one value from a lenghty dropdown list.

Cheers,

Sebastian

posts.author: Aaren (Aaren)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love this! The current dropdown, in terms of typical webform pattern, is more a multi-select field than a traditional drop-down. If I want a single-select drop-down, I’m forced to use the status column, which isn’t ideal since it confuses the use of this pattern across the system (e.g., colors have meaning, where drop-down items may not).

I’d suggest adding a “Column Settings” like “Date” that contains a “limit” field, since this settings pattern already exists within Monday and falls within the same user case.

posts.author: palmerijosh (Josh Palmeri)
posts.cookedHtml:
100000% needed! Thanks for posting this in the community, @sschreiner.

Josh

posts.author: PegaChris (Chris Maertz)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree that the “Dropdown” column, isn’t really a dropdown by typical convention. Allowing the ability to limit the selection would go a long ways towards making this more usable. In the current state, there is very little difference between tags & dropdowns yet there is a NEED for a TRUE DROPDOWN (like Status, but not having the significance, etc.).

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml: Currently all dropdowns are multi-select. Can you please add a setting to allow us to define whether its a single or multi-select dropdown? Kind of like how people has an unlimited/1/2/3 option. If dropdowns could have the same setting that be great.

posts.author: NodarDavituri (Nodar Davituri)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s really unbelievable that a basic single-select dropdown is not available in Work OS…

posts.author: MondayMaster (Ken Snyder)
posts.cookedHtml: Couldn’t you use a Status column in place of Drop-down in order to restrict selections to a single value?

posts.author: djbeame (Bernard Wilson)
posts.cookedHtml: from a functional point of view - yes, but visually it communicates a total different experience to a dropdown column.

posts.author: cberman (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for this, I want to limit a dropdown to 1 selection. Using Status feels like a workaround.
Being able to specify the dropdown selections are limited to 1, 2, 3, etc would be really cool.

posts.author: aholtzhauer (Andrew)
posts.cookedHtml: Very surprised this isn’t implemented yet. Yes, the “Status” column can be used as a workaround, but that only allows ~40 answers, and if you ever have a dropdown with more potential results, you’re stuck. Also the dropdown just feels like the natural input for certain columns - agreed with @cberman, being able to limit the dropdown amount to an entered number would be incredibly useful.

posts.author: amalec (Ashley)
posts.cookedHtml: Still no resolution to this? Starting to think voting on things is a waste of time.

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently, the Dropdown column seems to be set as a select one or more option. This is great in theory, as it make it up to the user to decide whether they want to select just one or many. But in use its a bit cumbersome. As it creates additional steps for the user when they want to change it from one thing to another. Rather than just selecting a different option and the selection being replaced. I need to now select another option, and then remove the previous selection.

Can we get the option to set a dropdown as a single select or multi-select type?

posts.author: tmrkk (Kristian Korsgaard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this should definitely be a thing. I think it should simply be an option in “Column Settings” like most of the other columns has.

“Column Settings” > “Selection method” > [“Single”, “Multiple”]

posts.author: jptcc (Juliana P)
posts.cookedHtml: any update from the developer on this? it’s so super useful to restrict dropdown to single selection esp for monday.forms

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml: I also agree that this should be a thing. At the moment, the workaround you can use is to use the Status column for single selections and the Dropdown columns for multiple selections.

posts.author: banemilkywaydeer (Michael A)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. It is a pretty basic requirement that things are a lot of the time, only one value.

posts.author: Eric_PBI (Eric Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: A workaround is to use the Label column instead. Dropdown column allows multiple, and Status is tied to status of the task. Label is independent and has only 1 option selectable.

posts.author: sean.jackson (Sean Jackson)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s disappointing that this is not already a part of the product. It’s fundamental. A List Box is equally fundamental.

posts.author: Croner (nigel croner zinyama)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this???

posts.author: marius84bv (Marius Munteanu)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you think you can tell us if you want to consider our request related to the dropdown column? For me, this change is very important, and from what I see, also for the users who wrote here about it

posts.author: swaffles (Sammy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m shocked this very basic feature is still not supported but Monday is adding AI features to the platform .

I have a picklist for US States – I don’t want anyone to select more than one and I can’t leverage the status or label columns because they have a limit of 40 values. Additionally, there’s a country column but not a state column. This is a frustrating scenario!

Does anyone know of any workarounds to this issue?

posts.author: whbraun (William Braun)
posts.cookedHtml:
We like that dropdown column can have a predefined list of values, but it also allows multiple value selections. We need the ability to define a list of values, but only accept the selection of a single value.

We considered using Status or Label column types, but they do not support conditional formatting of the cell.

posts.author: JWienecke (John Wienecke)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like a basic feature … and it should be easy to implement. The importance of this is significant as it makes a impact on the easy of use and the automations associated with this column.

posts.author: Han (Kuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com Team,

Will the functions be in the sprint plan of function addup?

posts.author: Susan26 (Susan Simmons)
posts.cookedHtml: Even SharePoint Lists have this feature. Please add this.

posts.author: bossavega (Stunday)
posts.cookedHtml: There are many threads that say the same thing. This is a basic feature. The moderator needs to consolidate the votes

posts.author: karel (Karel)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvoted - Just like John said, very simple but important. Does anyone know if this is coming soon?

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team this absolutely needs to happen ASAP. How do we get this rolling?

posts.author: Joe_Downey (Joe Downey)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-moderators @monday-team Can we at least get an update on plans for this from the devs please?

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dropdown fields absolutely need an option to force only one selection. Single-select, similar to how a status works but…a dropdown.

Currently, I am using a dropdown to list something we have more than 40 of (the largest number of options available in a single-select status column). I need to force the user to only select 1 option at a time.

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT

I appreciate my original request being moved here and rolled into a single thread, however, I’ve noticed some variation of this request has been submitted multiple times over the past year and a half. Are the votes from these other requests being added into this thread? I understand the voting system, but i feel like it should be less about votes and more about requests and comments overall. If it gets requested, then 50 people comment on various threads about it but only 10 people vote, does that count as 50 people asking for it or 10?
Also, this seems like something that should already be a part of the platform, and is easily attainable within the current framework of Monday and/or should already be in production.
Or do people have to rally others to vote in in a specific feature request thread like a political campaign?

This is one of those “gotta have it” things in Monday.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Vito,

I appreciate your comments and hear where you’re coming from.

At this time, the process is based off votes per feature request, a process that we believe works best for the structure of the community. We encourage users to upvote their desired feature requests (across all channels) and do our best to merge all matching feature requests to consolidate all votes - that said, we recognise some may fall through the cracks and appreciate this being brought to our attention. Our goal is to merge all matching feature requests into the one thread to ensure all votes are accurately counted. On that note, we understand that there can be variations across posts, and if the community member does not believe requests match, we will not go ahead with the merge, which is why you may find similar posts circulating the community…

Please find a more detailed overview of the process here → All you need to know about our new Feature Request process

There’s always room for improvement so I will ensure I take this my team internally

Regarding the feature request itself, I have reached out to the developers directly and asked whether there is information on a development status.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Update - the team have confirmed that the feature is in its consideration phase. No confirmed ETA at this stage, but it is on their radar! We appreciate your continued patience with our team.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community, Thank you for your thoughtful feedback. Our product team understands the importance having the capability to define how many values can be selected in a dropdown column and our product team is currently looking into different ways to bring this feature to life. While this feature is on our long-term roadmap, we do not have an exact date for when it will be released. We will update you as our team continues to make progress on this feature!

Keep sharing your ideas with us and learn more about our feature request process here:

All you need to know about our new Feature Request process Feature requests
Welcome to the feature request part of our community! This is your opportunity to get involved in our development process. Share and vote on features you would like to see implemented in the platform so we can build a roadmap together that best reflects your needs. How do you submit a feature request? First, check if your idea already exists in the "feature request" category and vote on that topic. That way, our team can see which features are most requested. You can use the search function o…

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette,

Is there someplace specific that shows all requests in the “long term roadmap”, as well as a generic status of that request in the process? This may be helpful information for Monday.com users to be able to see where their request is and have a general idea of how far away it may be.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
In 2024, our boards team is dedicated improving your boards experience, including making the auto id column more dynamic. Our product team understands the importance having the capability to define how many values can be selected in a dropdown column and so are working hard to release this feature very soon, hopefully in the next couple of months!

Our community will be the first to try out the top requested features, so make sure your notifications are turned on!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Vitro! Sorry for the delay here! You can use the filters at the top to filter long term roadmap and the different status. Go to the main feature page here and click on the filters above. Feature requests - monday Community Forum

Please note we just updated a lot of the status for 2024 so many of the features are now on the roadmap and have new updates :). You can also make sure to turn on notifications for the threads/ features that interest you the most to ensure you receive updates regarding their status. Check out: How to turn on notifications for a post

image1040×222 14.2 KB

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
Amber here from the product team. I’m very excited to announce that our team has been working on this feature thanks to your feedback and we’re looking to release it soon!

We know you’ve been waiting for this for a while, so I wanted to share a sneak peak until everything is ready for release:

posts.author: adinap (Adina Pierce)
posts.cookedHtml: This is great, hopefully it will work on subitem drop downs as well!

posts.author: adinap (Adina Pierce)
posts.cookedHtml: Tried it this morning, and it works on subitems! Thank you!! Looks a little different to your previous screenshot, but I’m happy.

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
Dropping in to let you know that we have already started gradually releasing this feature! If you’re one of the lucky users who already has access, I’d love to hear what you think! Which use cases are finally being addressed?
And if you don’t see the feature on your account just yet, hold tight- it’s on the way! Enjoy!!!

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @amber ,

Thanks for this update!
I can select single or multiple lables, but can’t see an option to limit the selection to defined number of selections.

Can you please clarify where can I limit the selection number?

Thank you ,
Oz

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @amber glad to see this coming! It’s not on my account yet, but I’m looking forward to having this capability.

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for finally implementing this functionality. I do have access to this feature in my account but as of right now when opting for “select multiple labels” I see no way yet to define what number or range of number of labels can be selected. Is that functionality still incoming?

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @sschreiner, @hbristol @Ozi @adinap
The feature was originally released with just a single select option. So if you’re still seeing this, hang tight! The full release that allows for specifying the number of labels should be released to all accounts very shortly.
Would be happy to hear your feedback!

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Great, will keep an eye out. Thanks for the note @amber

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just stumbled across the feature now rolled out to our account in the last day @amber
Looks great, works great! I like the messaging to the user when they try to go beyond the limit also.

…next Dropdown request, ability to import into Dropdown columns please!

Import into Dropdown Columns Feature requests
It would be immensely helpful to be able to import from Excel into dropdown columns. While upon first board setup you can import into a text column and then convert it to dropdown, that is not useful for the need to import repeatedly into an existing board. The lack of ability to do so has affected our board design in both CRM and in some of our Work Management boards. In CRM we will want to feed chunks of leads in on an ongoing basis in the future, and thus cannot use the Dropdown column eve…

Thanks!

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml: I wasn’t even aware that this is a current limitation. This will directly affect a set of boards we were about to build.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @sschreiner yes it’s something I’ve had to work around a fair amount. Please go vote for the feature request I linked to above, maybe we can get this additional Dropdown enhancement bumped up the list.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

I am excited to share that this feature is now released and you will be able to define how many values can be selected from the dropdown.

Thank you for sharing your feedback and helping bring this feature to life. It truly helps our team create a roadmap that best reflects the needs of our community!

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb thanks for the update! Great to have this feature!
